text.title: Swift Architecture as Tests via Harmonize + Swift Testing Core Rules
text.summary: ≥5 testable rules pinning the methodology's preconditions, output shape, and skip path. Each rule pairs a binary check with rationale + named source.

rule.statement: Architecture rules (layer boundaries, no-import constraints, naming, protocol conformance) MUST live as @Test/#expect cases using Harmonize on SwiftSyntax.
rule.rationale: Documented-but-not-tested rules erode; tests force enforcement.

rule.statement: CI MUST run `swift test` (or Xcode test plan) for every PR; arch tests are first-class.
rule.rationale: Without CI, arch tests are advisory; only failing the build holds the line.

rule.statement: Harmonize SPM dep MUST be pinned to an exact version; upgrades reviewed.
rule.rationale: Unpinned arch-test framework can change rule semantics silently.

rule.statement: Each arch test file MUST start with a doc-comment summarising the rule and the source (architecture decision record).
rule.rationale: Tests without rationale get deleted on red; documented tests survive.

rule.statement: Harmonize covers STRUCTURAL rules; SwiftLint covers STYLE. Both run; neither replaces the other.
rule.rationale: Harmonize can't catch line-length; SwiftLint can't catch import cycles.

rule.statement: If Swift < 5.9 (no Swift Testing) OR project too small to need arch rules OR team uses ArchUnit-like tool already, STOP.
rule.rationale: Forcing Harmonize on tiny / wrong-Swift-version repos creates churn.
